whatIsApiPlatform.question: What is an API Platform?
whatIsApiPlatform.answer: API Platform is a platform that allows developers and organizations to connect with Excise Department systems through APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) to securely access data and services with standardized protocols.

howToRegister.question: How do I register?
howToRegister.answer: You can register in 2 types: Individual for developers and small businesses, or Organization for government and enterprises. Click the "Register" button above to get started.

pricing.question: Is there a cost to use the platform?
pricing.answer: Basic registration is free, but there may be fees for certain services or usage exceeding quotas. Details will be shown for each API.

security.question: How secure is the platform?
security.answer: We use international security standards including OAuth 2.0, HTTPS, and data encryption. All API requests require authentication and are logged in audit trails.

support.question: Who do I contact for support?
support.answer: You can contact our support team at support@excise.go.th or check the documentation in the Developer Portal after registration.

home.title: Excise OpenAPI
home.subtitle: Excise Government Open API Platform
home.description: A Government Open API platform of the Excise Department for developers and partners to integrate excise-related services on a unified standard. It supports enterprise-grade security with identity and access management, enabling faster development, secure integration, and scalable service expansion for the future.
home.secureAuthDesc: Enterprise-grade authentication with OpenID Connect and OAuth2 standards.
home.advancedApisDesc: Comprehensive RESTful APIs for excise management and compliance reporting.
home.easyIntegrationDesc: Simple integration process with comprehensive documentation and support.
home.readyToStartDesc: Sign in to access the Excise OpenAPI portal and begin integrating with our APIs.

termsDialog.title: Terms and Conditions for User Registration
termsDialog.titleOrg: Terms and Conditions for Organization Registration
termsDialog.subtitle: Please read and accept the terms before proceeding with registration
termsDialog.intro: By registering for the Excise OpenAPI service of the Excise Department, you are indicating your intention to accept all of these Terms and Conditions of Service. Please read the following carefully before proceeding with registration. If you do not accept these Terms and Conditions, please exit the registration process immediately.

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms and Personal Data Protection Policy
section1.content1: Use of the Excise Department's internet services shall be subject to these Terms of Use, rules, regulations, procedures, and conditions, including any future amendments. The Excise Department has announced a Personal Data Protection Policy to inform you, as the data subject, and to obtain your consent for the collection, use, or disclosure of such personal data within the scope of the Excise Department's authority and mission.
section1.content2: Users will only be able to access the services after successfully completing registration as an internet service user. The Excise Department will implement appropriate security measures to prevent loss, unauthorized access, use, alteration, modification, or disclosure of personal data.
section1.content3: The Excise Department reserves the right to refuse or reject registration in certain cases, without prior notice or obligation to provide reasons, if the registration is deemed inconsistent with the intended purposes or may cause damage to the system and security of the Excise Department.
section1.content4: In the event of any updates or amendments to the Personal Data Protection Policy and these Terms of Service, the Excise Department will notify you through appropriate communication channels such as the website, email, or system notifications.

section2.title: 2. Personal Data Collection and Sources
section2.content1: The Excise Department will collect personal data, which includes any information that can identify you as the data subject, classified into two categories: (1) General personal data such as identification information (national ID number, name, title, address, organization name, phone number, email, gender) and work-related information, and (2) Sensitive data such as biometric data, face scan/recognition, religious information, and blood type appearing on national ID cards.
section2.content2: During registration and service usage, Users must provide personal and related information to the Excise Department that is accurate, truthful, and complete, and must keep such information up to date at all times. If any information changes, Users must update such information promptly.
section2.content3: The Excise Department may receive personal data directly from you, or from external agencies such as the Office of the Civil Service Commission, Customs Department, Revenue Department, Department of Provincial Administration, other relevant agencies, or from publicly available information.
section2.content4: The Excise Department will collect website visit data from all visitors through cookies or similar technologies on the website to provide convenience and better user experience, enable continuous login sessions, and study usage behavior to develop the website for easier, faster, and more efficient use. You can configure or delete cookies yourself through your web browser settings.
section2.item1: Full name and accurate contact information
section2.item2: National ID number or other acceptable identification
section2.item3: Address, phone number, and valid email address
section2.item4: Any other information requested by the Excise Department for service purposes

section3.title: 3. Purposes and Legal Basis for Personal Data Processing
section3.content1: The Excise Department will collect, use, or disclose your personal data for the following purposes: (1) Contract performance and contract fulfillment (2) Performance of duties in carrying out tasks for public interest or exercise of official authority vested in the Excise Department (3) Performance of legal obligations to which the Excise Department is subject (4) Legitimate interests of the Excise Department or other persons or juristic persons and (5) Other purposes for which consent has been obtained from the data subject.
section3.content2: The Excise Department will not disclose your personal data to external parties except when necessary for operational purposes, to comply with relevant laws, or when consent has been obtained from you. In cases where personal data must be disclosed to external parties, the Excise Department will enter into written agreements with data processors to ensure appropriate protection of personal data.
section3.content3: In cases where it is necessary to transfer or transmit personal data to foreign countries, the Excise Department will proceed in accordance with the criteria specified by law, which may consider: (1) The destination country has adequate personal data protection standards (2) Necessity under contract (3) Explicit consent from the data subject (4) Compliance with law or (5) Other reasons as specified by law.
section3.content4: Users must maintain the confidentiality of their username, password, API Key, and other authentication credentials, and exercise the highest degree of care to prevent unauthorized access or use by others. The Excise Department shall consider any actions performed through the User's account and password as actions carried out by the User.
section3.item1: Not disclosing passwords or credentials to any third party
section3.item2: Changing passwords periodically as required by the Excise Department
section3.item3: Immediately notifying the Excise Department of any suspected unauthorized account access
section3.item4: Accepting responsibility for all actions performed through the user's account

section4.title: 4. Data Retention Period and Deletion
section4.content1: The Excise Department will retain your personal data for the period necessary to achieve the purposes stated in these Terms, or for the period required by law. Generally, the Excise Department will retain personal data for a period of 5 years from the end of service usage, except in cases where law specifies otherwise, or for the purpose of establishing legal claims, compliance with or exercise of legal claims, or defense against legal claims, according to the statute of limitations for lawsuits and execution of judgments.
section4.content2: Upon expiration of the retention period, the Excise Department will proceed with appropriate steps to delete or destroy, or make personal data anonymous so that individuals cannot be identified. If the data subject requests deletion of personal data, the Excise Department will consider and proceed within 30 days from the date of receiving the request.
section4.content3: The Excise Department reserves the right to suspend service access, restrict account usage, or terminate user accounts immediately without prior notice if the User violates these Terms and Conditions, misuses the service, or engages in conduct that may damage the system, security, or reputation of the Excise Department.
section4.content4: The Excise Department reserves the right to delete user accounts that have remained inactive or have not been activated for a period exceeding 30 days from the date of registration, without prior notice, and shall not be liable for any damages arising from such deletion. Deleted accounts cannot be recovered under any circumstances.

section5.title: 5. Data Subject Rights
section5.content1: You, as the data subject, have the right to: (1) Right to withdraw consent in cases where consent was given for personal data processing (2) Right of access to your personal data and request copies of such data (3) Right to rectification to ensure accuracy or supplement incomplete personal data (4) Right to erasure of personal data under certain circumstances.
section5.content2: (5) Right to restriction of processing under certain circumstances (6) Right to data portability to transfer data provided to the Excise Department to another data controller or to yourself and (7) Right to object to personal data processing under certain circumstances. You can contact Excise Department officials to exercise these rights free of charge.
section5.content3: The Excise Department will consider and notify the result within 30 days from the date of receiving the request. In some cases depending on the circumstances, the Excise Department may not be able to proceed as requested, such as when it is necessary to perform duties under contract or law. However, in cases where consent has been given for personal data processing, you may withdraw such consent at any time.
section5.content4: If you have questions about the practical details of personal data protection operations, you can study the personal data protection guidelines specified at www.excise.go.th or contact Excise Call Center 1231 or email pdpa@excise.go.th. You also have the right to file complaints regarding non-compliance with personal data protection laws with the Expert Committee as specified in Section 87 of the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562.

section6.title: 6. Personal Data Security Measures
section6.content1: The Excise Department implements appropriate security measures for personal data, from limiting access to personal data only to persons with legitimate need-to-know for operational purposes, establishing information security policies, implementing appropriate technologies for data security, data encryption, preventing unauthorized data access, audit log monitoring, and providing training for relevant personnel and staff.
section6.content2: The Excise Department has established user management measures such as identity verification before data access, defining access rights according to roles and responsibilities, encrypting user passwords in databases, recording user activities for audit purposes, and periodic review of access rights. Additionally, there are physical controls such as restricting access to data storage areas, installing security systems in data storage locations, and controlling data removal from the organization.
section6.content3: Each user account is intended for personal use only and belongs exclusively to the registered account holder. Users are not permitted to transfer, lend, share, or allow any third party to use the account under any circumstances. If such actions are detected, the Excise Department reserves the right to immediately suspend or terminate the account without prior notice.
section6.content4: In cases where the Excise Department engages other persons or juristic persons as data processors, the Excise Department will enter into written agreements with essential provisions that processors must act only according to the Excise Department's instructions, implement appropriate security measures, notify the Excise Department of personal data breaches without delay, and not use or disclose personal data for purposes other than those specified in the agreement.

section7.title: 7. Personal Data Breach Management
section7.content1: In the event of a personal data breach, the Excise Department will proceed with clear and timely processes to ensure maximum protection of your personal data. The first step will be to investigate the breach upon discovery of any incident that may cause personal data leakage or unauthorized access, followed by risk assessment of the impact on your rights as the data subject.
section7.content2: If the incident is found to have high impact on the data subject, the Excise Department will notify the Personal Data Protection Committee Office (PDPC) within 72 hours as required by law to ensure compliance with the Personal Data Protection Act. After notification, if the breach poses high risk, you will be promptly notified along with recommendations for preventing impacts from the incident.
section7.content3: In all cases, when a personal data breach occurs, the Excise Department will record the incident in a Logbook or electronic system that can be audited to ensure remedial actions can be tracked and verified subsequently. The Excise Department will investigate to identify the cause of the breach and implement preventive measures to prevent recurrence of similar incidents.
section7.content4: You have a duty to immediately notify the Excise Department if you suspect unauthorized access to your account or personal data, or if you detect any abnormal behavior that may affect the security of personal data. Prompt notification will help the Excise Department prevent and resolve issues in a timely manner.

section8.title: 8. For Organizations
section8.content1: The registrant must have authority to act on behalf of the registered organization and must provide evidence of such authority to the Excise Department upon request, which may include power of attorney, certification letters, or documents showing position and responsibilities in the organization.
section8.content2: The organization shall be responsible for all actions of personnel and users associated with the organizational account, including API Key usage, data access, and any operations through the Excise OpenAPI system. The organization must implement appropriate internal controls to ensure that personnel and users strictly comply with these Terms and Conditions.
section8.content3: In cases where there are changes in authorized signatories or primary responsible persons of the organization, the organization must notify the Excise Department within 7 days from the date of change, along with submitting relevant supporting documents.
section8.content4: The organization must appropriately control and manage API Keys, not disclose or share API Keys with external parties or other organizations, and must immediately notify the Excise Department if API Keys are found to be disclosed or used without authorization.

finalSection.title: 9. Contact Information and Data Controller Details
finalSection.content1: Data Controller: Excise Department. Contact Address: 1448 Nakhon Chai Si Road, Thanon Nakhon Chai Si, Khlong San, Bangkok 10300. Phone: Excise Call Center 1231, 02-2821-5600-19. Email: pdpa@excise.go.th. Website: www.excise.go.th
finalSection.content2: Data Protection Officer (DPO): Director of Information Technology Center, Excise Department. Contact Address: Same as above. Phone: 02-2821-5600-19 ext. 24107. Email: pdpa@excise.go.th. If you have questions, complaints, or wish to exercise data subject rights, you can contact through the above channels.
finalSection.content3: Amendments to Terms: The Excise Department reserves the right to test and update this policy as deemed appropriate, with notification to users via the website or the email address provided. Continued use of the service after amendments to these Terms and Conditions constitutes acceptance of such amendments.
finalSection.content4: External Website Links: This Personal Data Protection Policy applies only to the use of the Excise Department's website. If there are links to other websites (even through channels on the Excise Department's website), users must study and comply with the personal data protection policy appearing on those respective websites.

termsDialog.agreeCheckbox: I have read, understood, and agreed to the above terms and conditions for registration as an internet service user of the Excise Department in their entirety.
termsDialog.heading: Terms and Conditions for User Registration
termsDialog.section1: 1. The use of the Excise Department's internet services shall be subject to its terms of use, rules, procedures, and conditions, including any future amendments. Access to the services shall only be granted after successful registration as an internet service user (hereinafter referred to as the "User"). The Excise Department reserves the right to refuse or reject any registration application in certain cases, without prior notice or the obligation to provide reasons.
termsDialog.section2: 2. In the course of registration and service usage, the User must provide personal and related information that is true, accurate, and complete. The User is responsible for keeping such information up to date at all times. If false, inaccurate, or incomplete information is provided, the Excise Department reserves the right to suspend or terminate the User's account as deemed appropriate.
termsDialog.section3: 3. Where the User is required to create a username and password for accessing the services, the User shall be responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of such credentials and shall exercise the highest degree of care to prevent unauthorized access or misuse. The Excise Department shall consider any actions performed through the User's account and password as actions carried out by the User, unless it can be proven that such actions resulted from a failure of the Excise Department's system.
termsDialog.section4: 4. The Excise Department reserves the right to suspend access to the service, temporarily restrict access to the account, or terminate the User's account without prior notice if the User is found to have violated or failed to comply with these terms and conditions, or if the User's actions may cause damage to the system, security, or the reputation of the Excise Department.
termsDialog.section5: 5. The Excise Department reserves the right to delete any User account that has remained inactive or has not been activated for a period exceeding 30 days from the date of registration, without prior notice, and shall not be responsible for any damages arising from such deletion.
termsDialog.section6: 6. The User's right to access and use the service shall immediately terminate upon deletion or termination of the User account for any reason whatsoever. In all cases, deleted accounts cannot be recovered, even if such deletion was unintentional or resulted from the User's own action or error. The User acknowledges and accepts this condition.
termsDialog.section7: 7. Each User account is intended for personal use only and belongs exclusively to the registered account holder. The User is not permitted to transfer, lend, share, or allow any third party to use the account under any circumstances. No third party may assume or take over the User account. If such actions are detected, the Excise Department reserves the right to immediately suspend or terminate the account without prior notice.

orgTermsDialog.title: Terms and Conditions for Organization Registration
orgTermsDialog.agreeCheckbox: I have read, understood, and agreed to the above terms and conditions for organization registration to use the Excise Department's API services in their entirety.
orgTermsDialog.section1: The registration of organizations to use the Excise Department's API services shall be subject to its terms of use, rules, regulations, procedures, and conditions as determined by the Excise Department, including any future amendments. Access to the services shall only be granted after successful registration and approval by the Excise Department. The Department reserves the right to refuse or reject any registration application without prior notice or the obligation to provide reasons. In this regard, the Excise Department, as the Data Controller under the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562, has a duty to comply with the criteria regarding the collection, use, or disclosure of personal data as stipulated by law.
orgTermsDialog.section2: The organization must provide information that is true, accurate, and complete, including official organization registration documents, legal entity information, designated contact person details with authority to act on behalf of the organization, and other supporting documents as required by the Excise Department. The organization is responsible for keeping such information up to date at all times and must promptly notify the Excise Department of any changes to the organization's information, including changes in the designated contact person. If false, inaccurate, or incomplete information is provided, or if the contact person lacks proper authority, the Excise Department reserves the right to suspend, revoke, or terminate access immediately as deemed appropriate. The organization shall be liable for any damages arising from the provision of false or inaccurate information or from actions by unauthorized persons.
orgTermsDialog.section3: The designated contact person shall be responsible for maintaining the confidentiality and security of all credentials, including API Key, Client ID, Client Secret, and other authentication credentials, and shall exercise the highest degree of care to prevent loss, unauthorized access, use, modification, alteration, or disclosure. The organization must implement appropriate security measures in accordance with Article 37 of the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562, which may include organizational measures, technical measures, and necessary physical measures. The organization shall be fully liable for any actions performed through its account, whether by the designated contact person, personnel, or any third party to whom access has been delegated.
orgTermsDialog.section4: The Excise Department reserves the right to suspend or terminate access to API services immediately without prior notice if the organization is found to have violated these terms and conditions, the API usage policy, applicable laws or regulations, engaged in fraudulent activities, used the services for illegal purposes, or if the organization's actions may cause damage to the system, data security, privacy rights of data subjects, or reputation of the Excise Department. In such cases, the organization shall be liable for all damages arising from such violations. The Excise Department also reserves the right to pursue legal action and seek compensation for any damages incurred as a result of such violations.
orgTermsDialog.section5: The organization shall use the API services only for the lawful purposes stated in the registration application and in accordance with the scope of use specified by the Excise Department. Any use beyond the stated purposes, use for commercial purposes different from those declared, or provision of services to third parties based on API data requires prior written approval from the Excise Department. The organization must not use the API services for illegal purposes, activities that infringe upon the rights of others, activities that violate public morals, or activities that may damage the reputation or credibility of the Excise Department. The organization must comply with usage limitations, rate limits, and access quotas as determined by the Excise Department for each API service and must not attempt to circumvent or bypass such limitations.
orgTermsDialog.section6: The organization agrees to strictly comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding data protection, privacy, and information security when using the API services, including but not limited to the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562, the Computer Crime Act, and the Cybersecurity Act. If the organization acts as a Data Processor or Data Controller of personal data obtained through the API services, the organization must implement appropriate security measures, establish clear procedures for handling personal data, and comply with data subjects' rights as required by law. The organization must not use personal data obtained through the API services for purposes other than those permitted by law without proper legal basis or consent from the data subjects.
orgTermsDialog.section7: The organization shall not transfer, sell, sublicense, lease, or allow any unauthorized third party to use the API credentials or services without prior written approval from the Excise Department. The organization must ensure that only authorized personnel with legitimate need-to-know have access to the credentials. The organization shall immediately notify the Excise Department within 72 hours of becoming aware of any suspected unauthorized access, security breaches, data breaches, loss of credentials, or any incidents that may affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of data or services. Such notification must include details of the incident, the scope of impact, preliminary cause analysis, and immediate remedial actions taken. The organization must cooperate fully with the Excise Department in investigating and resolving any security incidents and must implement corrective and preventive measures as recommended by the Department.

errors.changeFailed: Failed to change password. Please try again.

mfa.title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
mfa.pageDescription: Enhance account security by enabling multi-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized access
mfa.whyMfaBenefit1: Prevent unauthorized access even if credentials are compromised
mfa.whyMfaBenefit2: Mitigate risks from phishing and credential stuffing attacks
mfa.whyMfaBenefit3: Protect sensitive data and API access credentials
mfa.whyMfaBenefit4: Comply with ISO/IEC 27001 and information security best practices
mfa.authenticatorDesc: Use an authenticator application such as Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, or Authy to generate time-based 6-digit OTP codes that rotate every 30 seconds. This method presents lower risk than SMS-based authentication as it does not rely on cellular network availability.
mfa.disableWarningDesc: Disabling MFA will make your account more vulnerable to attacks.
mfa.disableConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to disable MFA?
mfa.disableWarning: MFA adds an extra layer of security to your account
mfa.recoveryCodesDesc: Recovery codes provide account access when your authenticator application is unavailable, such as device loss or application removal. Each code is valid for single use only.
mfa.setupDesc: Scan the QR code below to link your account with your Authenticator App
mfa.setupStep1: 1. Install Google Authenticator or Authy application
mfa.setupStep2: 2. Scan the QR code or enter the secret key manually
mfa.setupStep3: 3. Enter the 6-digit code from the application to verify
mfa.manualEntry: Or enter this secret key manually:
mfa.mfaEnabledSuccessDesc: Your account is now protected by an additional authentication factor
mfa.recoveryCodesWarning: Store these recovery codes in a secure location, such as a password manager or printed copy in a secure area. If you lose access to your authenticator and do not have these codes, account access cannot be recovered.
mfa.securityTipText: Do not store recovery codes on the same device where your authenticator application is installed
mfa.enterCodeFromApp: Enter the 6-digit authentication code from your Authenticator App
mfa.invalidCode: The code entered is invalid. Please verify and try again.
mfa.recoveryCodeDesc: Enter a recovery code that was provided when MFA was enabled
mfa.recoveryCodeWarning: Each recovery code is valid for single use only. Used codes are automatically invalidated.
mfa.invalidRecoveryCode: The recovery code entered is invalid
mfa.verifyError: Authentication failed. Please verify your code and try again.

sessions.title: Sessions & Activity
sessions.revokeAllConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to revoke all other sessions? This will sign you out from all other devices.

applications.title: Applications
applications.description: Manage your applications and API access

approvals.title: Pending Approvals
approvals.description: Review and process organization registration requests

approve.title: Confirm Approval
approve.whatWillHappen: What will happen when you approve:
approve.step1: Kratos identity will be created for the contact person
approve.step2: Welcome email will be sent with password reset link
approve.step3: Organization will gain access to the platform
approve.confirmQuestion: Are you sure you want to approve this registration?

reject.title: Reject Registration
reject.reasonRequired: Please provide a reason for rejection
reject.reasonMinLength: Reason must be at least 10 characters

requestInfo.title: Request More Information
requestInfo.messageMinLength: Message must be at least 20 characters

banner.title: We Value Your Privacy
banner.message: We use cookies to ensure the security and functionality of this government platform. Essential cookies are required for authentication and session management. You can customize your preferences for optional cookies.
banner.acceptAllAria: Accept all cookies including optional ones
banner.rejectOptionalAria: Use only essential cookies required for the platform
banner.customizeAria: Open cookie settings to customize your preferences
banner.govAssurance: Secured by Thai Government Standards - Your data is protected under PDPA

settings.title: Cookie Preferences
settings.intro: The Excise Department website uses cookies to provide efficient and secure services to users. Cookies help remember your visits and preferences to improve service quality and enhance your user experience. You can manage your cookie preferences according to your needs.

essential.desc: These cookies are strictly necessary for the website and security system to function. They are used for authentication, session management, and accessing secure areas of the website. Without these cookies, you will not be able to access critical platform services. These cookies are always enabled and cannot be disabled.

cookies.kratosSession: User authentication session (Ory Kratos) for maintaining login state and security

functional.desc: These cookies help the website remember your settings and preferences, such as display theme (light/dark mode), language selection (Thai/English), and timezone. They provide a convenient and personalized user experience tailored specifically for you. Disabling these cookies will require you to reconfigure settings each time you visit, but will not affect the website's core functionality.

analytics.desc: These cookies help the Excise Department understand visitor usage patterns and behaviors to analyze and improve service quality. The collected data is statistical and anonymous, unable to identify you personally. It is used solely to improve website functionality and user experience.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.consent: By accessing the Excise OpenAPI platform (www.excise.go.th/api-hub), you acknowledge and consent to the Excise Department's use of cookies or similar technologies to collect information about your website visits, in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) B.E. 2562.
intro.whatDesc: Cookies are small text files used to store information related to your platform access, such as date and time of visits, clicked links, pages visited, and various configuration settings. These files are stored on your computer and/or communication devices (such as notebooks, tablets, or smartphones) through your web browser when you access our platform. Cookies do not cause harm to your computer or communication devices.
intro.why: How the Excise Department Uses Cookies
intro.whyDesc: The Excise Department collects visitor information through cookies or similar technologies on websites under our management, or on your devices depending on the services used. We use cookies to improve efficiency in accessing and using the Excise Department's services via the internet for the following purposes: (1) To provide convenience and enhance user experience with our services, (2) To enable continuous login to your account on the Excise Department website, (3) To study website usage behavior for development to make the platform faster, easier to use, and more efficient.

strictlyNecessary.title: Strictly Necessary Cookies
strictlyNecessary.desc: These cookies are essential for the core functionality of the platform and cannot be disabled. They are used for authentication, security, and session management to ensure safe and reliable access to government services.
strictlyNecessary.why: Without these cookies, critical features such as logging in, accessing APIs, and maintaining secure sessions would not function properly.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.desc: These cookies enable enhanced features and convenience based on your preferences. You can disable these cookies without affecting core functionality, though your personalized experience may be reduced.
functional.why: These cookies improve your experience by remembering your theme preference (light/dark), language selection (English/Thai), and timezone settings across sessions.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.desc: These cookies collect anonymized information about platform usage patterns to help the Excise Department understand user behavior and improve service quality.
analytics.why: Analytics cookies are currently not implemented. When we introduce them in the future, they will be optional, fully transparent, and require your explicit consent.

managing.title: Managing Your Cookie Preferences
managing.desc: Data subjects can configure or delete cookie usage on their own through the settings in their web browser.
managing.option1: Use the Cookie Settings modal accessible from the footer of this platform to customize your preferences for functional and analytics cookies
managing.option2: Configure your web browser settings (Web Browser) to block, delete, or receive notifications about cookies
managing.option3: Note: Strictly Necessary Cookies cannot be disabled as they are required for platform security and functionality according to the PDPA B.E. 2562
managing.browserDesc: Most modern web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, etc.) allow you to view, manage, and delete cookies through their settings menu. Please visit your browser's help section for specific instructions on cookie management for your particular browser.
managing.impactDesc: Disabling Functional Cookies means your preferences (theme, language, timezone) will not be saved between sessions, and you will need to reconfigure them each time you visit the platform. Strictly Necessary Cookies cannot be disabled as they are essential for authentication and security - disabling them will prevent access to the platform entirely.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.desc: The Excise Department may update this Cookie Policy periodically to reflect changes in our practices, technology, or legal requirements. The "Last Updated" date at the top of this page indicates the most recent revision.
updates.notification: We will notify users of significant changes through platform announcements and email notifications to registered users.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc: If you have questions, concerns, or requests regarding our use of cookies or this Cookie Policy, please contact our Data Protection Officer:
contact.phone: Phone: Excise Call Center 1713 or +66 2 241 5600-19 ext. 64204, 64207, 64211
contact.office: Office: Excise Department, 1488 Nakhon Chai Si Road, Thanon Nakhon Chai Si, Dusit, Bangkok 10300, Thailand

intro.title: Introduction
intro.desc: The Excise Department, a government agency under the Ministry of Finance, is responsible for collecting excise taxes as government revenue from goods and services as specified in the tariff schedule attached to the Excise Tax Act B.E. 2560 (2017), as well as conducting inspections, prevention, and suppression of violations of excise tax laws. As a result, the Excise Department, in its capacity as a data controller and data processor under the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (2019) ("PDPA"), is obligated to comply with the principles regarding the collection, use, or disclosure of personal data as prescribed by law.
intro.commitment: This Privacy Policy is established under the authority of Section 32 of the Act on the Organization of Public Administration B.E. 2534 (1991), as amended by the Act on the Organization of Public Administration (No. 5) B.E. 2545 (2002), to serve as a guideline for the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data of users of the Excise Department API Platform in accordance with relevant regulations and procedures.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.name: Name
controller.nameValue: Excise Department, Ministry of Finance, Kingdom of Thailand
controller.addressValue: 1488 Nakhon Chai Si Road, Thanon Nakhon Chai Si, Dusit, Bangkok 10300, Thailand
controller.responsibilities: The Excise Department is the data controller responsible for the collection, processing, and protection of your personal data in accordance with the PDPA.

collection.title: Personal Data We Collect
collection.desc: We collect personal data directly from you during registration and when you use our services. The types of data we collect include:

categories.identityDesc: Full name, Thai Citizen ID, passport number (for foreign nationals), date of birth, nationality
categories.contactDesc: Email address, mobile phone number, physical address (province, district, sub-district, postal code)
categories.organizationDesc: Organization name, tax ID, business registration number, business type, contact person details
categories.technicalDesc: IP address, browser type, device information, API usage logs, access timestamps
categories.authenticationDesc: Username, password (encrypted), OAuth tokens, session information, OTP codes (temporary)

collection.children: We do not knowingly collect personal data from individuals under 20 years of age without parental consent as required by PDPA.

purposes.title: Purposes of Data Processing
purposes.desc: We process your personal data for the following lawful purposes:

list.1: Account Management: To create, maintain, and manage your user account and provide access to our API Platform
list.2: Authentication & Security: To verify your identity, authenticate access, prevent fraud, and protect against unauthorized use
list.3: Service Delivery: To provide API access, manage API credentials, process service requests, and deliver technical support
list.4: Communication: To send service notifications, security alerts, system updates, and respond to your inquiries
list.5: Compliance: To comply with legal obligations, government regulations, and audit requirements
list.6: Analytics & Improvement: To analyze platform usage, improve service quality, and develop new features (with your consent)
list.7: Audit & Accountability: To maintain audit trails for security monitoring, incident investigation, and regulatory compliance

legal.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legal.desc: We process your personal data based on the following legal grounds under the PDPA:
legal.consentDesc: You have given explicit consent for us to process your personal data for specific purposes (e.g., functional cookies, analytics)
legal.contractDesc: Processing is necessary to perform the contract between you and the Excise Department for API services
legal.legalObligationDesc: Processing is required to comply with Thai laws, government regulations, and official directives
legal.publicInterestDesc: Processing is necessary for the performance of public tasks carried out by the Excise Department

retention.title: Data Retention Period
retention.desc: The Excise Department will retain personal data of service users (data subjects) for the duration necessary to achieve the objectives outlined in this policy and to comply with legal requirements:
retention.activeDesc: Personal data is retained throughout the period of active account usage for providing platform services
retention.inactiveDesc: The Excise Department will retain personal data for a period of 5 years from the date of collection, except when retention is required to comply with laws, regulations, or when the Excise Department has a legitimate need to retain the data for establishing, exercising, or defending legal claims in accordance with the statute of limitations and enforcement laws
retention.auditDesc: Audit logs and security records related to platform access are retained for 5 years as required by government regulations and PDPA compliance requirements
retention.complianceDesc: Data required for legal compliance, tax enforcement, excise administration, dispute resolution, or enforcement of government agreements may be retained for extended periods as mandated by Thai law
retention.deletion: Upon expiry of the retention period, the Excise Department will implement appropriate procedures to delete or destroy personal data, or render it into anonymized data that cannot identify individuals.

rights.title: Your Rights Under PDPA
rights.desc: As a data subject, you have the following rights under the Personal Data Protection Act:
rights.accessDesc: Request access to your personal data and obtain a copy of the data we hold about you
rights.rectificationDesc: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data
rights.erasureDesc: Request deletion of your personal data when it is no longer necessary or when you withdraw consent
rights.restrictionDesc: Request restriction of processing under certain circumstances
rights.portabilityDesc: Receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format
rights.objectionDesc: Object to processing of your personal data for direct marketing or other purposes
rights.withdrawDesc: Withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing prior to withdrawal
rights.complaintDesc: Lodge a complaint with the Personal Data Protection Committee if you believe your rights have been violated
rights.exercise: To exercise these rights or submit complaints regarding personal data processing, please contact the Excise Call Center at 1713 or +66 2 241 5600-19 ext. 64204, 64207, 64211, or via email at pdpa@excise.go.th. The Excise Department will process your request promptly and in accordance with legal requirements. Data subjects also have the right to lodge complaints about non-compliance with personal data protection laws to the Expert Committee as specified in Section 73 of the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562.

security.title: Data Security Measures
security.desc: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, loss, or misuse:
security.encryptionDesc: All data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.3. Sensitive data at rest is encrypted using AES-256 encryption
security.accessDesc: Role-based access control (RBAC) ensures only authorized personnel can access personal data
security.authenticationDesc: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) and OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect protocols protect user accounts
security.monitoringDesc: 24/7 security monitoring, intrusion detection systems, and regular security audits
security.infrastructureDesc: Government-certified data centers in Thailand with physical and network security controls
security.incidentDesc: In case of a personal data breach, the Excise Department will follow clear and timely procedures to ensure maximum protection of your personal data. First, we will immediately investigate the breach when an event that may cause data leakage or unauthorized access is detected. Then, we will assess the risk to the rights of data subjects. If the event has a high impact on data subjects, we must notify the Office of the Personal Data Protection Commission (OPDPC) within 72 hours as required by law to ensure compliance with the PDPA. After notifying the OPDPC, if the impact assessment shows high risk, data subjects will be notified as soon as possible along with recommendations to prevent impacts from the incident. In all cases of personal data breaches, the Excise Department will record the incident in a Logbook or electronic system that can be audited to enable corrective actions to be tracked and verified subsequently
security.trainingDesc: Regular training for all staff handling personal data on privacy and security best practices

sharing.title: Data Sharing and Third Parties
sharing.desc: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. We may share your data only in the following limited circumstances:
sharing.serviceDesc: We use trusted third-party service providers who process data on our behalf:
sharing.oryKratos: Ory Kratos & Hydra: Identity and access management (data stored in Thailand)
sharing.minio: MinIO: Secure object storage for documents (data stored in Thailand)
sharing.governmentDesc: We may share data with other government agencies when required by law or for official purposes
sharing.legalDesc: We may disclose data when required by law, court order, or to protect our rights and safety
sharing.dpa: All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements ensuring PDPA compliance and equivalent security standards.

international.title: International Data Transfers
international.desc: Your personal data is stored and processed exclusively within the Kingdom of Thailand in government-certified data centers.
international.noTransfer: We do not transfer personal data outside Thailand without your explicit consent and appropriate safeguards as required by PDPA Section 28.
international.sovereigntyDesc: As a Thai Government agency, we ensure all citizen data remains under Thai jurisdiction and protection.

cookies.title: Cookies and Tracking
cookies.desc: We use cookies and similar technologies as described in our Cookie Policy.
cookies.linkEnd: for detailed information about the cookies we use and how to manage them.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.desc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, legal requirements, or service offerings.
updates.notification: Material changes will be communicated via:
updates.banner: Prominent notice on the platform homepage
updates.effective: Updated policy will be effective 30 days after notification, unless immediate compliance is required by law
updates.review: We encourage you to review this policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your data.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or your personal data, please contact:
contact.department: Information Security Expert, Information Technology Center, Excise Department
contact.address: 1488 Nakhon Chai Si Road, Thanon Nakhon Chai Si, Dusit, Bangkok 10300, Thailand
contact.phone: Phone: Excise Call Center 1713 or +66 2 241 5600-19 ext. 64204, 64207, 64211

pdpa.title: PDPA Compliance Statement
pdpa.desc: This Privacy Policy complies with the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (2019) and follows the guidelines of the Personal Data Protection Committee of Thailand.
pdpa.commitment: The Excise Department is committed to upholding the highest standards of data protection and respecting your privacy rights.
pdpa.certified: Our data protection practices are regularly audited and certified by government authorities.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.desc: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the Excise OpenAPI platform ("Platform"), operated by the Excise Department of Thailand ("we", "us", or "our"). By accessing or using the Platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
intro.acceptance: If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Platform.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.bindingDesc: By creating an account, accessing the Platform, or using our API services, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy.
acceptance.capacityDesc: You represent that you are at least 20 years of age and have the legal capacity to enter into these Terms. If you are accessing the Platform on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms.
acceptance.modificationsDesc: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes will be effective 30 days after posting unless immediate compliance is required by law. Your continued use of the Platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the modified Terms.

service.title: Service Description
service.platformDesc: The Excise OpenAPI provides government API services for developers, businesses, and organizations to access official excise-related data and services from the Excise Department of Thailand.
service.scope: Services include but are not limited to:

list.1: API access for excise tax information and calculations
list.2: Business registration and licensing data integration
list.3: Real-time data synchronization with government systems
list.4: Developer tools, documentation, and sandbox environments

service.availabilityDesc: We strive to maintain 99.9% uptime but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. The Platform may be unavailable due to maintenance, updates, or circumstances beyond our control.

accounts.title: User Accounts
accounts.registrationDesc: You must register for an account to access API services. You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and to update this information as necessary.
accounts.responsibilityDesc: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must immediately notify us of any unauthorized access or security breach.
accounts.individual: Individual Account: For personal developers and individual users
accounts.organization: Organization Account: For businesses, NGOs, and government agencies requiring team access and higher API quotas
accounts.terminationDesc: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or for any reason at our discretion with reasonable notice.

acceptable.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptable.compliance: You agree to use the Platform only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree NOT to:

prohibited.1: Violate any applicable laws, regulations, or third-party rights
prohibited.2: Use the Platform for fraudulent, malicious, or unauthorized purposes
prohibited.3: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Platform, other accounts, or connected systems
prohibited.4: Reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract source code from our APIs
prohibited.5: Transmit viruses, malware, or any harmful code through the Platform
prohibited.6: Abuse, harass, or threaten other users or our staff
prohibited.7: Use automated tools to scrape, harvest, or collect data beyond API rate limits
prohibited.8: Resell, redistribute, or sublicense API access without written authorization
prohibited.9: Misrepresent your identity or affiliation with any person or organization
prohibited.10: Use the Platform to compete with or replicate our services

acceptable.enforcement: Violation of this Acceptable Use Policy may result in immediate account suspension or termination and may be reported to law enforcement authorities.

api.title: API Usage Terms
api.limitsDesc: Each account tier has specific rate limits and monthly quotas. Exceeding these limits may result in temporary throttling or service suspension. Contact us for enterprise quotas.
api.credentialsDesc: API keys and access tokens are confidential. You must not share, publish, or expose your credentials. Compromised credentials must be regenerated immediately.
api.dataDesc: API responses are provided for your authorized use only. You may cache data for up to 24 hours but must respect data freshness requirements specified in API documentation.
api.attributionDesc: When displaying data obtained from our APIs, you must provide clear attribution: "Data provided by Excise Department of Thailand" with a link to our Platform.
api.monitoringDesc: We monitor API usage for performance, security, and compliance. Suspicious patterns may trigger automatic rate limiting or account review.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property Rights
intellectual.ownershipDesc: All content, APIs, documentation, and services provided through the Platform are owned by the Excise Department of Thailand and protected by Thai and international intellectual property laws.
intellectual.licenseDesc: We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Platform and APIs solely for your authorized purposes under these Terms.
intellectual.restrictions: You may not claim ownership of, modify, create derivative works from, or remove any proprietary notices from our Platform or APIs.
intellectual.userContentDesc: You retain ownership of any content, applications, or integrations you create using our APIs. By using the Platform, you grant us a license to use your feedback and suggestions for improvement.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.asIsDesc: The Platform and APIs are provided "as-is" and "as-available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.
liability.noWarrantyDesc: We do not warrant that the Platform will be uninterrupted, error-free, secure, or free from viruses or other harmful components. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of data provided through APIs.
liability.damagesDesc: To the maximum extent permitted by Thai law, we shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits, revenue, data, or use arising from your use of the Platform.
liability.maxLiabilityDesc: Our total liability for any claims arising from these Terms or your use of the Platform shall not exceed the amount you paid to us (if any) in the 12 months preceding the claim.
liability.governmentDesc: As a government agency, the Excise Department operates under sovereign immunity to the extent provided by Thai law.

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.desc: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Excise Department of Thailand, its officers, employees, and agents from any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including legal fees) arising from:

list.1: Your violation of these Terms or any applicable laws
list.2: Your use or misuse of the Platform or APIs
list.3: Your applications, content, or integrations that use our APIs
list.4: Infringement of any third-party rights by your use of the Platform
list.5: Any disputes between you and other users or third parties

termination.title: Termination
termination.byYouDesc: You may terminate your account at any time by contacting us at contact@excise.go.th. Termination does not relieve you of obligations incurred before termination.
termination.byUsDesc: We may suspend or terminate your access immediately without notice if you violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or for operational or legal reasons.
termination.effectDesc: Upon termination, your right to access the Platform ceases immediately. All API credentials will be revoked. Provisions regarding intellectual property, liability, indemnification, and governing law survive termination.
termination.dataDesc: After account termination, we will retain your data as required by law or our retention policies. You may request data deletion subject to legal and operational requirements.

governing.title: Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
governing.lawDesc: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Kingdom of Thailand, without regard to conflict of law principles.
governing.jurisdictionDesc: Any disputes arising from these Terms or your use of the Platform shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Thailand located in Bangkok.
governing.negotiationDesc: Before initiating legal proceedings, parties agree to attempt to resolve disputes through good-faith negotiation for at least 30 days.
governing.languageDesc: These Terms are provided in English and Thai. In case of discrepancies, the Thai version shall prevail for legal purposes.

general.title: General Provisions
general.entireDesc: These Terms, together with our Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy, constitute the entire agreement between you and the Excise Department regarding the Platform.
general.waiverDesc: Our failure to enforce any provision of these Terms does not constitute a waiver of that provision or any other provision.
general.severabilityDesc: If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
general.assignmentDesc: You may not assign or transfer your rights under these Terms without our written consent. We may assign our rights without restriction.
general.noticesDesc: Official notices will be sent to the email address associated with your account. You must keep your contact information current.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: For questions, concerns, or support regarding these Terms of Service, please contact:
contact.department: Excise Department, Ministry of Finance, Kingdom of Thailand
contact.address: 1488 Nakhon Chai Si Road, Thanon Nakhon Chai Si, Dusit, Bangkok 10300, Thailand

acknowledgment.title: Acknowledgment
acknowledgment.desc: By using the Excise OpenAPI platform, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy.

track.title: Track Registration
track.subtitle: Check the status of your organization registration

error.notFound: Registration not found. Please check your tracking code and try again.
error.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please try again later.
error.unknown: An error occurred while fetching your registration. Please try again.
error.network: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.
error.help: If you continue to experience issues, please contact our support team.

timeline.title: Application Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Track the progress of your registration

help.title: Need Help?
help.description: If you have any questions about your registration, please don't hesitate to contact us.

dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.topApisDesc: Most active APIs in the last 24 hours
